wp_001.title: Transport Strike
wp_001.body: Unions announce a nationwide transport strike starting Monday. The images are everywhere.

choices.A: Negotiate and ease up.
choices.B: Stand firm: minimum service and toughness.
choices.C: Deploy military buses to keep the country moving.

wp_002.title: Climate Summit
wp_002.body: An international climate summit is taking place on your soil. Everyone expects a strong announcement.

choices.A: Announce an ambitious plan with green investments.
choices.B: Set gradual goals with review clauses.
choices.C: Make a big symbolic statement, with no numbers.

wp_003.title: Cyberattack on Hospitals
wp_003.body: Hospitals see their systems locked down. A ransom is demanded and the media talk about chaos.

choices.A: Pay discreetly to restore things quickly.
choices.B: Refuse to pay and mobilize a cyber task force.
choices.C: Accuse a rival country without public proof.

wp_004.title: Historic Match
wp_004.body: The national team reaches a historic final. Your reaction is expected.

choices.A: Support the celebration and invest in grassroots sports.
choices.B: Stay restrained: short speech, no spending.
choices.C: Organize a giant parade starting tomorrow.

wp_005.title: Severe Seasonal Flu
wp_005.body: A virulent flu is overwhelming emergency rooms. Experts want to act fast.

choices.A: Launch a vaccination and prevention campaign.
choices.B: Wait: 'no need to panic.'
choices.C: Impose targeted temporary restrictions.

wp_006.title: Sharp Currency Drop
wp_006.body: The currency is plunging on the markets. Imports cost more, and the opposition is accusing you of amateurism.

choices.A: Announce a stability plan and cut spending.
choices.B: Inject massive aid and reassure everyone.
choices.C: Seek a support agreement with allies.

wp_007.title: Natural Disaster
wp_007.body: An exceptional storm hits the coast. Cities are asking for immediate emergency help.

choices.A: Send the army for emergency assistance.
choices.B: Unlock a special fund and direct aid.
choices.C: Let the regions handle it, with the state only coordinating.

wp_008.title: Corruption Scandal
wp_008.body: A video shows an adviser accepting a 'gift.' Social media is on fire.

choices.A: Fire them immediately and open an investigation.
choices.B: Defend them: 'editing and exaggeration.'
choices.C: Create a distraction with a surprise reform.

wp_009.title: Joining an Alliance
wp_009.body: A small neighboring state wants to join your defense alliance. This could upset some partners.

choices.A: Support membership quickly.
choices.B: Delay it: study and strict conditions.
choices.C: Refuse to avoid tensions.

wp_010.title: Coffee Shortage
wp_010.body: A coffee shortage is driving up prices. Cafés are putting up 'rationing' signs and the country is complaining loudly.

choices.A: Temporarily lower import taxes.
choices.B: Launch a 'national tea' campaign with official humor.
choices.C: Strategic stockpile: buy in bulk now.

wp_011.title: Tax Data Leak
wp_011.body: Tax records are circulating online. Many are accusing the state of negligence.

choices.A: Be transparent: admit it and fix it fast.
choices.B: Minimize it: 'nothing serious.'
choices.C: Create a stronger national cyber agency.

wp_012.title: Mega-Factory Investment
wp_012.body: An industrialist offers a mega-factory that would create jobs… but it uses a lot of energy.

choices.A: Accept it with strict environmental standards.
choices.B: Accept it with no conditions to move fast.
choices.C: Reject it and bet on local small businesses.

wp_013.title: Border Incident
wp_013.body: A non-lethal incident occurs at the maritime border. The media are talking about humiliation.

choices.A: Respond firmly with increased patrols.
choices.B: Propose a bilateral commission of inquiry.
choices.C: Ignore it to cool things down.

wp_014.title: Weather Satellite Hack
wp_014.body: A weather satellite goes down after an intrusion. Forecasts become unreliable.

choices.A: Invest in a new satellite quickly.
choices.B: Rely temporarily on allied data.
choices.C: Turn the incident into an ambitious space project.

wp_015.title: Pension Reform
wp_015.body: A pension reform is on the table. It is impossible to please everyone.

choices.A: A soft and gradual reform.
choices.B: A fast and strict reform.
choices.C: Postpone it by a year and open consultations.

wp_016.title: Rising Energy Prices
wp_016.body: Energy bills are climbing. Households want relief, and companies are threatening to relocate.

choices.A: A temporary price shield.
choices.B: Targeted aid only for the most vulnerable.
choices.C: An energy import agreement with partners.

wp_017.title: Massive Network Outage
wp_017.body: A massive outage cuts internet access for hours. People ask you, 'who protects the country?'

choices.A: A national digital resilience plan.
choices.B: Publicly punish the operator.
choices.C: Militarize part of the critical infrastructure.

wp_018.title: Global Cultural Festival
wp_018.body: A global cultural festival wants to settle in your country. Guaranteed prestige, enormous costs.

choices.A: Fund it heavily to shine.
choices.B: Moderate support + private partnerships.
choices.C: Refuse: national emergencies come first.

wp_019.title: Arrival of Climate Refugees
wp_019.body: An island is devastated by rising waters. Refugees are asking for asylum.

choices.A: Welcome them with an integration plan and aid.
choices.B: Welcome them temporarily, then push for international relocation.
choices.C: Refuse and send aid on site instead.

wp_020.title: Tech Demonstration
wp_020.body: A startup proposes a drone delivery network. It could boost the economy, but scare the public.

choices.A: Allow it with strict rules and test zones.
choices.B: Allow it everywhere: 'we innovate!'
choices.C: Ban it: calm and safety come first.

wp_021.title: Housing Crisis
wp_021.body: Rents are exploding. Young people are protesting: 'we can't live anymore.'

choices.A: A massive building plan and aid.
choices.B: Strong rent regulation.
choices.C: Let the market adjust with tax incentives.

wp_022.title: State Dinner Incident
wp_022.body: At a state dinner, your minister makes a bad joke and offends a guest. It is already front-page news.

choices.A: Immediate official apologies.
choices.B: Minimize it: 'misunderstood humor.'
choices.C: Reshuffle: replace the minister on the spot.

wp_023.title: Piracy Offshore
wp_023.body: Cargo ships are being threatened by pirates in a maritime zone. Shipping companies demand a response.

choices.A: Send a frigate as an escort.
choices.B: Negotiate an international surveillance coalition.
choices.C: Pay for subsidized private protection.

wp_024.title: Teachers' Strike
wp_024.body: Teachers are threatening to block exams. Parents are panicking.

choices.A: Raise salaries and soften the reform.
choices.B: Keep the reform, promise bonuses later.
choices.C: Replace classes with emergency online learning.

wp_025.title: AI in Government
wp_025.body: You are offered AI to speed up administrative procedures. Efficient, but worrying.

choices.A: Roll it out as a pilot with mandatory human oversight.
choices.B: Deploy it at scale to save time.
choices.C: Refuse: 'we keep the human touch.'

wp_026.title: Highly Publicized Military Exercise
wp_026.body: A giant military exercise is proposed to show the country's strength. Neighboring states are watching.

choices.A: Do it, but invite foreign observers.
choices.B: Do it without inviting anyone: clear message.
choices.C: Cancel it to avoid tensions.

wp_027.title: Star Scientist
wp_027.body: A national researcher wins a global prize. You are being asked to increase the research budget.

choices.A: Increase it sharply: scholarships + laboratories.
choices.B: Increase it a little, but communicate a lot.
choices.C: Congratulate her and move on.

wp_028.title: Waste Crisis
wp_028.body: Landfills are saturated. Mayors are threatening to close sites. Everyone wants the solution somewhere else.

choices.A: Invest in recycling and modern centers.
choices.B: Temporarily export waste through an agreement.
choices.C: Ration and raise fines immediately.

wp_029.title: Wave of Fires
wp_029.body: A series of fires is ravaging forests. The images are playing on loop.

choices.A: Strengthen firefighters' resources and prevention.
choices.B: Mobilize the army to support emergency services.
choices.C: Requisition private resources, a controversial measure.

wp_030.title: Tempting Trade Deal
wp_030.body: A trade deal promises jobs, but requires regulatory concessions.

choices.A: Sign with safeguard clauses.
choices.B: Sign right away, without changes.
choices.C: Refuse and protect the domestic market.

wp_031.title: Space Launch
wp_031.body: A national space mission can be launched. Success means prestige, failure means global mockery.

choices.A: Launch with maximum safety, even if it costs more.
choices.B: Launch fast to be first.
choices.C: Delay and cooperate with a partner.

wp_032.title: Stadium Scandal
wp_032.body: A new stadium is accused of costing twice as much as planned. The images are everywhere.

choices.A: Public audit and penalties.
choices.B: Own it: 'it's an investment.'
choices.C: Turn it into a national sports museum to save face.

wp_033.title: Supply Tensions
wp_033.body: An essential component is missing. Several factories may have to shut down.

choices.A: Subsidize local production.
choices.B: Negotiate priority access with partners.
choices.C: Build costly strategic stockpiles.

wp_034.title: Leak of a Bill
wp_034.body: A bill leaks before the announcement. Rumors take over.

choices.A: Publish the full text and explain it.
choices.B: Say it is fake and open an internal investigation.
choices.C: Speed things up and submit the bill tomorrow.

wp_035.title: Mystery Drones Case
wp_035.body: Unknown drones are flying over sensitive sites. Conspiracy theories are exploding.

choices.A: Transparent communication + technical investigation.
choices.B: Temporarily ban civilian drones.
choices.C: Total silence: discreet operation.

wp_036.title: Bread Prices
wp_036.body: The price of bread suddenly rises. Bakers talk about costs, citizens talk about injustice.

choices.A: Targeted aid to artisans + anti-speculation controls.
choices.B: Let it happen: 'free market.'
choices.C: General subsidy to lower prices.

wp_037.title: Tourism Crisis
wp_037.body: Tourism collapses after a series of viral bad images. Hotels are calling for help.

choices.A: International campaign + lower tourism taxes.
choices.B: National support only, no external image push.
choices.C: Do nothing: 'it will bounce back.'

wp_038.title: National Service Program
wp_038.body: An expanded civic service program is proposed: useful, but some see it as a burden.

choices.A: Highly encouraged volunteer system with benefits.
choices.B: Short mandatory service for everyone.
choices.C: Drop it and fund nonprofits instead.

wp_039.title: Water Crisis
wp_039.body: A drought threatens several regions. Farmers are demanding quick decisions.

choices.A: Restrictions + investments in irrigation.
choices.B: Priority to industry to preserve the economy.
choices.C: Buy water through temporary international agreements.

wp_040.title: Surveillance Debate
wp_040.body: Security services want more surveillance powers against vague threats. Citizens are worried.

choices.A: Increase powers with independent oversight.
choices.B: Increase them without oversight: efficiency first.
choices.C: Refuse and invest in social prevention.

wp_041.title: Global Arms Expo
wp_041.body: A global arms fair wants to come to your country. The economy benefits, but the image could suffer.

choices.A: Host it with a strict ethics charter.
choices.B: Host it without conditions: major contracts.
choices.C: Refuse: favor a peaceful image.

wp_042.title: Tax Reform
wp_042.body: A tax reform is proposed. Simple on paper, explosive in the streets.

choices.A: Cut taxes for lower incomes, offset elsewhere.
choices.B: Cut taxes for everyone and cut spending.
choices.C: Raise taxes on the highest incomes.

wp_043.title: Fake News Epidemic
wp_043.body: A wave of fake news is spreading confusion before a major announcement. Some want restrictions.

choices.A: Create an independent public fact-checking unit.
choices.B: Fast law against disinformation, tough version.
choices.C: Let it circulate: 'freedom of speech.'

wp_044.title: Embassy Outage
wp_044.body: A friendly embassy suffers a strange power outage on your soil. The ally wants guarantees.

choices.A: Immediate investigation + stronger protection.
choices.B: Diplomatic apologies + compensation.
choices.C: Say it was a routine incident, nothing more.

wp_045.title: Major Rail Project
wp_045.body: A rail project would connect two struggling regions. Expensive, but symbolic.

choices.A: Launch the project with public funding.
choices.B: Launch it with a public-private partnership.
choices.C: Cancel it and only renovate what already exists.

wp_046.title: Military Recruitment Crisis
wp_046.body: The army is struggling to recruit. Generals are talking about a future 'gap.'

choices.A: Raise pay and improve conditions.
choices.B: A massive patriotic campaign.
choices.C: Reduce military ambitions and invest elsewhere.

wp_047.title: Statue Controversy
wp_047.body: An old statue is dividing residents between tradition and modernity. The media are turning the matter into a permanent controversy.

choices.A: Organize a local referendum.
choices.B: Quietly move the statue to a museum.
choices.C: Ignore it and talk about something else.

wp_048.title: Consulate Incident
wp_048.body: A national consulate abroad is targeted by vandals. Public opinion demands a reaction.

choices.A: Officially protest and demand reparations.
choices.B: Send a reinforced security team.
choices.C: Stay discreet to avoid escalation.

wp_049.title: Medicine Crisis
wp_049.body: A shortage of a common medication is causing concern. Pharmacies are displaying 'out of stock.'

choices.A: Subsidize fast national production.
choices.B: Buy emergency supplies abroad.
choices.C: Ration it and reserve it for priority cases.

wp_050.title: Year-End Speech
wp_050.body: Your year-end speech is approaching. The country wants concrete action… and a little hope.

choices.A: A very factual speech: numbers and a clear plan.
choices.B: A unifying and humorous speech.
choices.C: A martial speech: 'hard times, unity.'

wp_051.title: Hot Mic Scandal
wp_051.body: As you leave a meeting, your mic stays on. An awkward sentence is heard by everyone.

choices.A: Own it and laugh at yourself publicly.
choices.B: Say it was out of context and attack the media.
choices.C: Announce an anti-bureaucracy reform to regain control.

wp_052.title: Overloaded Strategic Port
wp_052.body: The country's biggest port is overloaded. Thousands of containers are blocking supply chains.

choices.A: Invest urgently in logistics and docks.
choices.B: Temporarily requisition military resources.
choices.C: Negotiate alternative routes with partners.

wp_053.title: Bank on the Brink
wp_053.body: A major bank is showing signs of weakness. Rumors alone are enough to cause panic.

choices.A: Guarantee deposits and rescue the bank.
choices.B: Let it fail: 'the market decides.'
choices.C: Force a merger with a stronger player.

wp_054.title: Peace Conference
wp_054.body: You are asked to host a peace conference between two countries that openly hate each other.

choices.A: Accept and offer active mediation.
choices.B: Accept but remain neutral and discreet.
choices.C: Refuse: too politically risky.

wp_055.title: Fuel Prices
wp_055.body: Fuel prices are rising. Drivers are threatening to block the roads.

choices.A: Temporarily cut taxes.
choices.B: Targeted aid + public transport plan.
choices.C: Do nothing: 'transition is necessary.'

wp_056.title: Black Series of Accidents
wp_056.body: Several serious accidents hit public infrastructure. People are saying the country is cracking apart.

choices.A: A national infrastructure renovation plan.
choices.B: Audit + sanctions + crisis communication.
choices.C: Mobilize army engineers to fix things fast.

wp_057.title: Visa Crisis
wp_057.body: Visa delays are exploding. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Simplify procedures with a digital portal.
choices.B: Tighten things up: 'security first.'
choices.C: Fast-track reciprocity agreements with a few countries.

wp_058.title: Massive Water Leak
wp_058.body: A major water leak leaves a large city without drinking water. Images of empty bottles are causing panic.

choices.A: Unlock an emergency plan and launch mass distribution.
choices.B: Impose immediate strict restrictions.
choices.C: Request logistical help from allies.

wp_059.title: Hard-Hitting Anti-Smoking Campaign
wp_059.body: The ministry proposes a very tough anti-smoking campaign. Effective or excessive?

choices.A: Launch the campaign + quitting support.
choices.B: A softer version: less shocking, more educational.
choices.C: Drop it: too many complaints expected.

wp_060.title: Defense Industry Restart
wp_060.body: Your defense factories are running slowly. Experts say a strong country must produce.

choices.A: Invest to restart national production.
choices.B: Buy abroad: faster and cheaper.
choices.C: Industrial cooperation with allies.

wp_061.title: Comedian Controversy
wp_061.body: A comedian mocks the government and public TV wants to cancel his appearance. Your reaction is expected.

choices.A: Let it happen: freedom of speech.
choices.B: Cancel it: preserve authority.
choices.C: Invite the comedian to a debate, but it is risky.

wp_062.title: Roller-Coaster Stock Market
wp_062.body: The stock market becomes highly unstable. Small savers panic, while major investors already seem to benefit from it.

choices.A: Reassure people + financial stability measures.
choices.B: Tax part of speculative trading.
choices.C: Do not intervene: 'free markets.'

wp_063.title: Police Equipment
wp_063.body: Law enforcement is asking for modern equipment. Pressure is rising.

choices.A: Modernize with training and independent oversight.
choices.B: Modernize fully, no debate: efficiency.
choices.C: Delay it and invest in mediation/prevention.

wp_064.title: Ballooning Deficit
wp_064.body: The deficit is exceeding forecasts. Economic partners want a credible path forward.

choices.A: A gradual savings plan + targeted investments.
choices.B: Fast and strict cuts.
choices.C: Negotiate delays and outside support.

wp_065.title: Alert on Imported Tech
wp_065.body: Experts warn that imported equipment may contain flaws. Fear of espionage is spreading.

choices.A: Replace it with a national solution.
choices.B: Immediately ban the suspicious equipment.
choices.C: Negotiate guarantees and audits with the supplier.

wp_066.title: Rail Crisis
wp_066.body: The rail network keeps breaking down. Memes are calling it the 'ghost train.'

choices.A: A major modernization plan.
choices.B: Partial privatization to move faster.
choices.C: Buy trains abroad in an emergency.

wp_067.title: Mandatory Service Debate
wp_067.body: Some propose a short 'citizen' draft. Others call it archaic.

choices.A: A voluntary version with benefits.
choices.B: A short obligation to strengthen defense.
choices.C: Refuse and invest in a professional army.

wp_068.title: Agricultural Crisis
wp_068.body: A bad season puts farmers in trouble. Prices rise, and so does anger.

choices.A: Direct aid + an agricultural transition plan.
choices.B: Open imports to lower prices.
choices.C: Negotiate a trade deal to support exports.

wp_069.title: Leak of a Secret Report
wp_069.body: An internal report criticizes your strategy. It leaked. Your opposition is thrilled.

choices.A: Transparency: acknowledge it and adjust.
choices.B: Hunt the mole and stay firm.
choices.C: Counterattack: publish a more flattering report.

wp_070.title: National Aircraft Takeoff
wp_070.body: A locally designed aircraft is about to make its maiden flight. Success means pride, failure means worldwide shame.

choices.A: Maximum safety, even if delayed.
choices.B: Launch fast: symbolism first.
choices.C: Cooperate with a partner to reduce the risk.

wp_071.title: Massive Demonstration
wp_071.body: A huge demonstration is planned. The leaders want to meet with you before Saturday.

choices.A: Meet the leaders and offer a compromise.
choices.B: Hold the line and secure downtown.
choices.C: Propose a major national public debate.

wp_072.title: Regional Defense Deal
wp_072.body: You are offered a regional defense agreement. Some see protection, others provocation.

choices.A: Sign: stronger deterrence.
choices.B: Sign, but with de-escalation clauses.
choices.C: Refuse and favor diplomacy.

wp_073.title: Minor Radioactive Alert
wp_073.body: A minor leak is reported at an energy site. Nothing dramatic… but public opinion is panicking.

choices.A: Full transparency + stronger inspections.
choices.B: Minimal communication to avoid panic.
choices.C: Temporarily shut down the site.

wp_074.title: Rent Boom
wp_074.body: Rents are soaring in the big cities. Many young people say they can barely afford housing anymore.

choices.A: Rent caps + construction.
choices.B: Tax incentives for landlords + construction.
choices.C: Do not intervene: 'market.'

wp_075.title: Global E-Sports Tournament
wp_075.body: An e-sports tournament wants to settle in your capital. Some call it genius, others wasteful.

choices.A: Support it and promote digital innovation.
choices.B: Private support only, no public money.
choices.C: Refuse: priorities lie elsewhere.

wp_076.title: Unemployment Rising Again
wp_076.body: Unemployment is rising again. The media want a clear plan, and fast.

choices.A: Training plan + public investment.
choices.B: Lower payroll charges for companies.
choices.C: A major long-term reindustrialization plan.

wp_077.title: Offended Alliance
wp_077.body: A historic ally feels you ignored them. They threaten to 'reassess the relationship.'

choices.A: Direct call + strong symbolic gesture.
choices.B: Stay firm: 'we do not beg.'
choices.C: Economic compensation through a trade deal.

wp_078.title: Rising Drug Trafficking
wp_078.body: Trafficking numbers are up. The public wants results, not speeches.

choices.A: Strengthen police + international cooperation.
choices.B: Public health approach + prevention.
choices.C: A massive and spectacular operation.

wp_079.title: Prison Escape
wp_079.body: A spectacular prison escape takes place. People say, 'if even prisons are escaping, where is this country going?'

choices.A: Prison security reform + more resources.
choices.B: Immediate sanctions and reshuffle.
choices.C: Stronger reintegration plan, controversial measure.

wp_080.title: Tech Pact
wp_080.body: A partner country proposes a technology pact: patent sharing and joint research.

choices.A: Sign: speed up innovation.
choices.B: Sign but protect sensitive sectors.
choices.C: Refuse to keep national control.

wp_081.title: Airline Crisis
wp_081.body: A national airline is in trouble. Saving it would be expensive.

choices.A: Save it with strict conditions.
choices.B: Let it fail: competition.
choices.C: Temporarily nationalize it.

wp_082.title: National Celebrations Under Tension
wp_082.body: National celebrations are approaching. Risk of unrest, but banning them would spark anger.

choices.A: Keep them with reinforced security.
choices.B: Cancel them out of caution.
choices.C: Keep them but heavily limit and supervise them.

wp_083.title: Dispute Over a Treaty
wp_083.body: An economic treaty is being challenged in parliament. Your camp is divided.

choices.A: Renegotiate some points to bring people together.
choices.B: Force it through: authority.
choices.C: Withdraw the text and start over.

wp_084.title: Blood Donation Crisis
wp_084.body: Blood stocks are low. Hospitals are warning of a serious but manageable situation.

choices.A: Major national campaign + incentives.
choices.B: A sober appeal, without incentives.
choices.C: Ask allies for logistical help.

wp_085.title: Tension in a Maritime Zone
wp_085.body: Ships are staring each other down in a strategic maritime zone. Each side is testing the other.

choices.A: Stronger patrols and deterrence.
choices.B: Immediate talks and de-escalation.
choices.C: Coalition with allies for a joint presence.

wp_086.title: Justice Reform
wp_086.body: The justice system is judged too slow. Some want more resources, others more severity.

choices.A: More resources + simplified procedures.
choices.B: More severity + faster sentencing.
choices.C: A major participatory overhaul, but it will take time.

wp_087.title: Cultural Boycott
wp_087.body: A movement is calling for a boycott of a cultural event linked to a foreign partner. A dilemma.

choices.A: Keep the event: culture above tensions.
choices.B: Cancel it: avoid controversy.
choices.C: Turn it into a broader international event.

wp_088.title: Fishermen's Crisis
wp_088.body: Fishermen are protesting: quotas, costs, competition. They threaten to block the ports.

choices.A: Aid + international quota talks.
choices.B: Temporary subsidies without negotiating.
choices.C: Get tougher: controls and sanctions on illegal fishing.

wp_089.title: Doctor Shortage
wp_089.body: Some regions lack doctors. Residents say, 'we live far away, we die fast.'

choices.A: Bonuses + public health centers.
choices.B: Mass teleconsultation + incentives.
choices.C: Temporary service obligation for new graduates.

wp_090.title: Leak of a Military Prototype
wp_090.body: A military prototype appears on forums. Technical details are circulating. Embarrassing.

choices.A: Quiet investigation + strengthen internal security.
choices.B: Communicate: minimize and muddy the waters.
choices.C: Speed up the program: 'we will not be slowed down.'

wp_091.title: National Cinema Crisis
wp_091.body: National cinema is losing ground. Some are asking for a plan to preserve culture.

choices.A: Public support + cultural exports.
choices.B: Limited aid, economy first.
choices.C: Partnerships with foreign platforms.

wp_092.title: Contested Defense Budget
wp_092.body: Your defense budget is increasing. Part of the population thinks the money should go elsewhere.

choices.A: Maintain it + explain the strategy and be transparent.
choices.B: Reduce it and invest in social programs.
choices.C: Maintain it but offset it with an economic plan.

wp_093.title: Scientific Trust Crisis
wp_093.body: A non-fatal medication controversy makes trust collapse. Experts are angry, and so is the public.

choices.A: Create a public independent commission.
choices.B: Defend the institutions without compromise.
choices.C: Change the rules and tighten approvals.

wp_094.title: An Island Wants More Autonomy
wp_094.body: An island in the country is demanding more autonomy. Debates are heating up, but you want to avoid a fracture.

choices.A: Negotiate a more autonomous status.
choices.B: Refuse outright: national unity.
choices.C: Propose a supervised referendum.

wp_095.title: Sports Diplomacy
wp_095.body: A friendly match with a rival could ease relations. Or trigger a scandal at the first problem.

choices.A: Organize the match and manage security.
choices.B: Refuse to avoid a diplomatic incident.
choices.C: Do a joint cultural event instead.

wp_096.title: Startup Crisis
wp_096.body: Startups complain: taxes, red tape, lack of investors. They threaten to leave.

choices.A: Simplify things + innovation tax credit.
choices.B: Targeted aid to strategic sectors.
choices.C: Let it happen: only the best survive.

wp_097.title: Airport Incident
wp_097.body: A security incident delays thousands of flights. Public opinion is furious, and so are diplomats.

choices.A: Strengthen security + official apologies.
choices.B: Publicly sanction management.
choices.C: Delegate to a specialized private agency.

wp_098.title: Economic Summit
wp_098.body: A global economic summit wants a clear direction: growth, redistribution, or caution.

choices.A: Announce a massive pro-investment plan.
choices.B: Announce strict budget discipline.
choices.C: Announce a balanced compromise, a moderate position.

wp_099.title: Civil Nuclear Program
wp_099.body: A civil nuclear program is proposed for energy independence. The issue is deeply divisive.

choices.A: Launch it with transparency and strict controls.
choices.B: Launch it fast and hard: sovereignty first.
choices.C: Give it up and invest in other energy sources.

wp_100.title: Awkward Diplomatic Gift
wp_100.body: A partner gives you a giant gift. Public opinion cries propaganda, but refusing would be delicate.

choices.A: Accept it and offer a cultural gift in return.
choices.B: Politely refuse: 'thank you, but no.'
choices.C: Accept it and display it to the public.

wp_101.title: Bank Card Outage
wp_101.body: Card payments go down. Lines grow longer and shopkeepers panic.

choices.A: Guarantee compensation for shopkeepers.
choices.B: Requisition the operator to restore service urgently.
choices.C: Let the central bank handle it without communicating.

wp_102.title: Oceans Summit
wp_102.body: A summit on ocean protection wants a strong announcement. Fishermen and NGOs are watching you.

choices.A: Create ambitious marine protected areas.
choices.B: Gradual measures + aid to sectors.
choices.C: A symbolic speech, no constraints.

wp_103.title: Civil Aviation Incident
wp_103.body: A technical problem forces a plane to make an emergency landing. Nothing serious, but fear is spreading.

choices.A: Aviation safety audit + total transparency.
choices.B: Tighten safety, minimal communication.
choices.C: Publicly sanction the airline.

wp_104.title: Driver's License Reform
wp_104.body: Getting a driver's license is considered too expensive and too long. Young people want immediate change.

choices.A: Cut costs through aid and simplification.
choices.B: Digitize and toughen it: fewer failures, more control.
choices.C: Touch nothing: 'other priorities.'

wp_105.title: Near-Health Crisis
wp_105.body: A mild but very contagious virus is spreading. Companies fear massive absenteeism.

choices.A: Prevention + recommended remote work.
choices.B: No guidance: 'normal life.'
choices.C: Strict temporary measures in enclosed spaces.

wp_106.title: Regional Budget Overrun
wp_106.body: A region overspends its budget and asks the state to cover the gap. Other regions cry unfairness.

choices.A: Help, but with conditions and an audit.
choices.B: Refuse: local responsibility.
choices.C: Create a national fund to prevent crises.

wp_107.title: Debate on the Official Language
wp_107.body: A movement wants to strengthen the national language in government. The debate divides the country.

choices.A: Strengthen it with education and exceptions.
choices.B: Impose it strictly, with no exceptions.
choices.C: Refuse: openness comes first.

wp_108.title: Threatened Trade Route
wp_108.body: An international trade route becomes unstable. Insurers are raising rates.

choices.A: Escort cargo ships with the navy.
choices.B: International security coalition.
choices.C: Go around it: new routes, even if more expensive.

wp_109.title: Private Tutoring Crisis
wp_109.body: Private tutoring is exploding and widening inequalities. Parents want a solution.

choices.A: Stronger public tutoring support.
choices.B: Education voucher for low-income families.
choices.C: Let it happen: 'freedom of choice.'

wp_110.title: Leak of a Defense Plan
wp_110.body: An internal defense document leaked. Nothing classified, but the image of control takes a hit.

choices.A: Investigation + modernize procedures.
choices.B: Accuse a foreign actor without public proof.
choices.C: Minimize it and move on.

wp_111.title: E-Waste Crisis
wp_111.body: Electronic waste is piling up. The media call it 'the mountain of old phones.'

choices.A: Recycling plan + trade-in bonus.
choices.B: Tax new devices to fund sorting.
choices.C: Temporary international export agreement.

wp_112.title: Duel of Heads of State
wp_112.body: A rival head of state gently provokes you in public. Cameras are waiting for your response.

choices.A: Reply with humor and defuse it.
choices.B: Reply firmly to show authority.
choices.C: Ignore it and publish a sober statement.

wp_113.title: Traffic Jam Crisis
wp_113.body: Traffic jams are reaching record levels. Companies talk about huge losses.

choices.A: Invest in public transport and carpooling.
choices.B: Roll out urban tolls, though unpopular.
choices.C: Quickly build additional lanes.

wp_114.title: Heat Record
wp_114.body: An unusual heat wave hits the country. Hospitals are warning about vulnerable people.

choices.A: Heatwave plan + cooling centers.
choices.B: Let cities handle it alone.
choices.C: Exceptional measures: adjusted hours, restrictions.

wp_115.title: Cultural Industry in Decline
wp_115.body: Performance venues are emptying out. Artists are asking for a support plan.

choices.A: Public support + cultural exports.
choices.B: Private partnerships + targeted aid.
choices.C: Refuse: 'the market will decide.'

wp_116.title: Public Service Reform
wp_116.body: You are offered a plan to merge several public agencies to save money. Unions are already protesting.

choices.A: Gradual merger with social guarantees.
choices.B: Fast and strict merger.
choices.C: Cancel it and invest to improve things instead.

wp_117.title: Home Insurance Crisis
wp_117.body: Home insurance is skyrocketing in some areas. People are saying, 'we can no longer afford coverage.'

choices.A: Create a public reinsurance mechanism.
choices.B: Targeted aid to the hardest-hit areas.
choices.C: Do not intervene: competition.

wp_118.title: Favoritism Scandal
wp_118.body: A public tender seems to have favored someone close to a minister. The case risks becoming a scandal.

choices.A: Cancel the tender + investigation.
choices.B: Defend the process: 'everything is legal.'
choices.C: Replace the minister and move on.

wp_119.title: School Uniform Debate
wp_119.body: Some schools want to impose uniforms. People talk about equality… or nostalgia.

choices.A: Allow it locally, as an experiment.
choices.B: Impose it nationwide.
choices.C: Refuse, individual freedom.

wp_120.title: Space Agreement
wp_120.body: A partner proposes a space agreement: data sharing, joint missions. The military wants it.

choices.A: Sign: open cooperation.
choices.B: Sign but classify a strategic part.
choices.C: Refuse to keep control of space.

wp_121.title: Student Housing Crisis
wp_121.body: Student housing is running short. Universities fear a chaotic academic year.

choices.A: Fast plan: modular housing.
choices.B: Financial aid to students, no construction.
choices.C: Partner with the private sector to build fast.

wp_122.title: Spare Parts Shortage
wp_122.body: Garages are running short of spare parts. Delays are exploding, taxi drivers are complaining, everyone is complaining.

choices.A: Relocate part of production.
choices.B: Priority import agreement.
choices.C: Strategic stockpiles, costly but safe.

wp_123.title: Municipal Police Crisis
wp_123.body: Mayors are asking for more local security resources. Associations fear escalation.

choices.A: More resources + oversight and training.
choices.B: More resources without debate.
choices.C: Invest in prevention and mediation.

wp_124.title: Scientific Treaty
wp_124.body: A scientific treaty promises breakthroughs, but involves sharing sensitive data.

choices.A: Sign with strict safeguards.
choices.B: Sign immediately: trust.
choices.C: Refuse: risk too high.

wp_125.title: Street Artists Crisis
wp_125.body: Street performers find the rules too strict and threaten to mobilize.

choices.A: Loosen the rules and create dedicated zones.
choices.B: Keep them: public order first.
choices.C: Create a paid permit, controversial measure.

wp_126.title: Bridge Alert
wp_126.body: A major bridge is showing signs of fatigue. Closing it means chaos, keeping it open means risk.

choices.A: Partially close it and repair it fast.
choices.B: Keep it open and monitor heavily.
choices.C: Build an emergency alternative.

wp_127.title: Import Quota Conflict
wp_127.body: Industrial groups want to limit imports they consider unfair. Your partners threaten retaliation.

choices.A: Limit them with exceptions and negotiations.
choices.B: Limit them brutally.
choices.C: Refuse and compensate with internal aid.

wp_128.title: Gastronomy Festival
wp_128.body: A major gastronomy festival could boost tourism. But it costs a fortune… in butter.

choices.A: Fund it: global showcase.
choices.B: Moderate support + private sponsors.
choices.C: Refuse: not a priority.

wp_129.title: Urban Fire Crisis
wp_129.body: Fires of unknown origin hit several neighborhoods. Rumors are going in every direction.

choices.A: Investigation + more firefighters and prevention.
choices.B: Massive and visible security operation.
choices.C: Minimal communication to avoid panic.

wp_130.title: Rumor of a Gas Shortage
wp_130.body: A simple rumor triggers long lines at gas stations. Nothing is confirmed… but panic is already here.

choices.A: Communicate immediately and release stockpiles.
choices.B: Let it pass: it will calm down.
choices.C: Temporarily limit purchases per vehicle.

wp_131.title: Major Fiber Buildout
wp_131.body: A national fiber plan is ready. It could boost the economy, but it is expensive and disrupts roads.

choices.A: Launch the full plan now.
choices.B: Roll it out in stages to spread the cost.
choices.C: Let the private sector do it without support.

wp_132.title: Non-Aggression Pact
wp_132.body: A rival country proposes a non-aggression pact. Some see a trap, others an opportunity.

choices.A: Sign with checks and mechanisms.
choices.B: Refuse: weakness perceived.
choices.C: Sign quickly to calm the markets.

wp_133.title: Customs Reform
wp_133.body: Customs is accused of being too slow. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Modernize with technology and hiring.
choices.B: Max out inspections.
choices.C: Simplify by reducing some inspections.

wp_134.title: Taxi Crisis
wp_134.body: Taxi drivers are protesting a new mobility app. Users just want to get there on time.

choices.A: Negotiate a compromise and fair rules.
choices.B: Support innovation: let the app operate.
choices.C: Temporarily block the app.

wp_135.title: Cyberbullying at School
wp_135.body: A scandal involving online harassment shakes the country. Parents demand concrete measures.

choices.A: Prevention plan + support units.
choices.B: Stricter laws + quick penalties.
choices.C: Hold platforms accountable through an agreement.

wp_136.title: Incident at Sea
wp_136.body: A national ship is aggressively inspected by a foreign power. The images are everywhere.

choices.A: Protest and send an escort.
choices.B: Negotiate calmly through diplomacy.
choices.C: Respond with economic sanctions.

wp_137.title: Library Crisis
wp_137.body: Libraries are closing earlier due to budget cuts. Citizens launch the hashtag #BookOrNothing.

choices.A: Increase the local cultural budget.
choices.B: Digitize massively and reduce hours.
choices.C: Look for private sponsors.

wp_138.title: Space Tourism
wp_138.body: A company wants to launch space tourism from your territory. Huge prestige, risks, and debate.

choices.A: Allow it with ultra-strict standards.
choices.B: Allow it to attract investment.
choices.C: Refuse to avoid a major accident.

wp_139.title: Counterfeit Crisis
wp_139.body: Counterfeits are exploding. Brands are furious, consumers are getting fooled.

choices.A: Strengthen customs + international cooperation.
choices.B: Fast law and heavy penalties.
choices.C: Education campaign + targeted action.

wp_140.title: Work Hours Reform
wp_140.body: A proposal would make work more flexible. Some applaud, others call it precariousness.

choices.A: Flexibility with strong protections.
choices.B: Broad flexibility to boost the economy.
choices.C: Refuse: preserve the current model.

wp_141.title: Embassy Incident
wp_141.body: A protester throws paint on a foreign embassy. The ally demands an apology.

choices.A: Official apologies + stronger security.
choices.B: Say it was isolated: no apologies.
choices.C: Punish harshly to show accountability.

wp_142.title: Medication Price Crisis
wp_142.body: The price of some medicines is rising. Associations demand immediate regulation.

choices.A: Temporarily cap some prices.
choices.B: Targeted aid for the most vulnerable.
choices.C: Negotiate with the industry in exchange for advantages.

wp_143.title: Prisoner Exchange
wp_143.body: A prisoner exchange is proposed with a rival. Public opinion is divided.

choices.A: Accept quietly and with guarantees.
choices.B: Refuse: principle of authority.
choices.C: Accept publicly as a gesture of peace.

wp_144.title: Major Reforestation Plan
wp_144.body: A reforestation plan could improve the country's image and the environment, but some industrial groups are worried.

choices.A: Ambitious plan + green jobs.
choices.B: Moderate and voluntary plan.
choices.C: Drop it: economy first.

wp_145.title: Health Care Hiring Crisis
wp_145.body: Hospitals are struggling to recruit. Care workers want concrete measures, not applause.

choices.A: Raise pay and improve conditions.
choices.B: One-time bonus + reorganization.
choices.C: Recruit internationally through agreements.

wp_146.title: Debate on Cameras
wp_146.body: Some mayors want more cameras in the streets. Others fear widespread surveillance.

choices.A: Deploy them with independent oversight and transparency.
choices.B: Deploy them massively: safety first.
choices.C: Refuse and fund social prevention.

wp_147.title: Science Teacher Crisis
wp_147.body: There is a shortage of science teachers. Students are falling behind, industry is worried.

choices.A: Recruitment plan + bonuses.
choices.B: National online classes as backup.
choices.C: Business-university partnership.

wp_148.title: Incident at the Economic Summit
wp_148.body: At an economic summit, a translation mistake creates a misunderstanding. The other delegation reacts badly.

choices.A: Apologize immediately and correct it.
choices.B: Minimize it: 'technical detail.'
choices.C: Offer an economic concession to calm things down.

wp_149.title: Mineral Water Crisis
wp_149.body: A popular mineral water brand is suspected of a quality problem. Social media is panicking.

choices.A: Preventive recall + transparent investigation.
choices.B: Quiet checks, no announcement.
choices.C: Immediate penalty and firm communication.

wp_150.title: Grand Speech on Power
wp_150.body: You are asked for a speech on the country's 'power.' Some want dreams, others pragmatism.

choices.A: Emphasize diplomacy and stability.
choices.B: Emphasize defense and sovereignty.
choices.C: Emphasize the economy and innovation.

wp_151.title: Speech Too Long
wp_151.body: Your speech runs too long, and the footage spreads everywhere.

choices.A: Give a short, self-mocking speech the next day.
choices.B: Blame the technical team for sabotaging the speech
choices.C: Launch a reform to make public speeches shorter and clearer.

wp_152.title: A Summit… About Cooking
wp_152.body: An international summit is organized… by a cooking show. The chefs want a photo with you.

choices.A: Go and sign a symbolic 'gastronomic pact.'
choices.B: Send your minister: you are officially working.
choices.C: Refuse: 'diplomacy is not done with a ladle.'

wp_153.title: Drones Over the Parade
wp_153.body: Amateur drones are flying over an official parade. Security is stressed, the public is fascinated.

choices.A: Ban them strictly and strengthen surveillance.
choices.B: Create authorized zones + simple registration.
choices.C: Invite drone clubs into a national tech program.

wp_154.title: Payment Outage
wp_154.body: A bug blocks electronic payments for 6 hours. People rediscover… coins.

choices.A: Cyber emergency plan + targeted compensation.
choices.B: Minimize it: 'minor incident,' then move on.
choices.C: Invest massively in resilient national infrastructure.

wp_155.title: Awkward Ambassador
wp_155.body: An ambassador makes a joke that falls flat. Very flat. Geopolitical level: floor tile.

choices.A: Official apologies + diplomatic gesture.
choices.B: Recall them immediately: zero tolerance.
choices.C: Create a joint cultural event.

wp_156.title: Presidential Train Delayed
wp_156.body: Your official train arrives late. The 'punctuality' slogan from your campaign comes back from the archives.

choices.A: Own it publicly and announce a serious rail plan.
choices.B: Blame the unions: immediate showdown.
choices.C: Partially privatize operations to speed things up.

wp_157.title: Museum Strike
wp_157.body: Museums close in protest. Tourists are taking pictures of… the doors.

choices.A: Increase the culture budget and negotiate quickly.
choices.B: Stay firm: economy first.
choices.C: Create a public-private fund and a governance reform.

wp_158.title: National Robot Lawnmower
wp_158.body: An innovation program proposes a national lawn-mowing robot. The project mostly attracts mockery.

choices.A: Subsidize it: tech showcase.
choices.B: Cancel it: it looks too much like a gadget.
choices.C: Refocus on useful robots for hospitals and logistics.

wp_159.title: Chocolate Shortage
wp_159.body: A logistics crisis causes a chocolate shortage. Citizens call it a 'moral red line.'

choices.A: Secure supply through fast agreements.
choices.B: Launch accelerated local production, but it is costly.
choices.C: Temporarily ration it and bet on transparency.

wp_160.title: Festival Under Protection
wp_160.body: A major festival is threatened by security risks. Organizers want a decision tomorrow.

choices.A: Keep it with reinforced security and a clear plan.
choices.B: Cancel it: 'zero risk.'
choices.C: Keep it but reduce attendance and coordinate with allies.

wp_161.title: Startups in Exile
wp_161.body: Startups are saying, 'we are leaving.' They even made t-shirts. Very bad sign.

choices.A: Massively simplify and lower some taxes.
choices.B: Targeted aid only for strategic sectors.
choices.C: Stay firm: 'we do not govern under blackmail.'

wp_162.title: Border of Trolls
wp_162.body: A wave of disinformation is targeting your country. The trolls are better organized than your schedule.

choices.A: Create an independent anti-disinformation unit + transparency.
choices.B: Hard cyber response and diplomatic sanctions.
choices.C: Media literacy campaign + platform partnerships.

wp_163.title: Delivery Strike
wp_163.body: Delivery workers stop working. Citizens rediscover the word 'walking.'

choices.A: Mediation + fast social agreements.
choices.B: Limited requisition for essential goods.
choices.C: Speed up logistics automation, though unpopular.

wp_164.title: Leaking Budget
wp_164.body: An accounting mistake reveals a major budget gap.

choices.A: Gradual savings plan + public audit.
choices.B: Immediate and strong cuts.
choices.C: Economic stimulus to offset it through growth.

wp_165.title: Ship Too Close
wp_165.body: A foreign military ship comes too close. The tone stays calm, but tension rises.

choices.A: Strengthen naval presence and deterrence.
choices.B: Direct call and diplomatic de-escalation.
choices.C: Coalition with allies for joint patrols.

wp_166.title: Operation 'Clean City'
wp_166.body: Cities are complaining about waste. The media launch the hashtag #NationalTrash.

choices.A: National cleanliness plan + local jobs.
choices.B: Stronger sanctions and inspections.
choices.C: Private partnership to modernize collection.

wp_167.title: Passport Redesign
wp_167.body: The current passport is considered too easy to forge, and it is becoming a real image and security problem.

choices.A: New national biometric passport, but it is costly.
choices.B: Quick solution bought abroad.
choices.C: Co-development with allies + shared standards.

wp_168.title: Ruined Stadium
wp_168.body: A major stadium is supposed to host a final, but the inspection reveals that it is not in good enough condition.

choices.A: Express renovation funded by the state.
choices.B: Move the event abroad, which would be humiliating.
choices.C: Public-private partnership + fast repairs.

wp_169.title: Selfie Diplomacy
wp_169.body: A head of state absolutely wants a selfie with you. Your staff says: 'buzz trap.'

choices.A: Accept and frame the message around friendship.
choices.B: Politely refuse: strict protocol.
choices.C: Accept, but only in exchange for a concrete deal.

wp_170.title: Souvenir Inflation
wp_170.body: Tourist souvenir prices are exploding. Tourists are leaving with… photos.

choices.A: Temporarily lighten local taxes.
choices.B: Controls and caps in some areas.
choices.C: Support local crafts to increase supply.

wp_171.title: Cyberattack on Hospital
wp_171.body: A hospital is hit by a cyberattack. The staff has to return to paper records, showing how outdated the system has become.

choices.A: Health cyber plan + massive investment.
choices.B: Cyber retaliation + international sanctions.
choices.C: Outsource security to a specialized player.

wp_172.title: Teacher Shortage
wp_172.body: Some schools are missing teachers. Parents are improvising classes… in DIY crafts.

choices.A: Bonuses + recruitment + accelerated training.
choices.B: Mass digital support, though unpopular.
choices.C: Long-term reform: revalued career, high cost.

wp_173.title: Peace Summit… in Virtual Reality
wp_173.body: A platform proposes a diplomatic summit in virtual reality. Advantage: no jet lag.

choices.A: Test it: diplomatic innovation.
choices.B: Refuse: too gimmicky, too risky for your image.
choices.C: Accept, but in a secure hybrid format.

wp_174.title: Power Outage
wp_174.body: An outage hits several regions. People bring out candles and conspiracy theories.

choices.A: Grid plan + modernization, though expensive.
choices.B: Immediate energy-saving measures, though unpopular.
choices.C: Temporary energy import agreements with partners.

wp_175.title: Air Traffic Controllers' Strike
wp_175.body: Air traffic controllers are threatening to strike. The country is preparing to rediscover trains. Again.

choices.A: Negotiate and invest in modernization.
choices.B: Minimal requisition for essential flights.
choices.C: Partially automate in the medium term.

wp_176.title: Housing Bubble
wp_176.body: Housing prices are rising too fast. Access to housing is becoming more and more difficult.

choices.A: Build more + targeted aid for first-time buyers.
choices.B: Stricter rent and transaction controls.
choices.C: Let the market breathe: less intervention.

wp_177.title: Military Base to Modernize
wp_177.body: A strategic base is aging badly. One soldier says, 'the coffee machine is our main weapon.'

choices.A: Modernize quickly, with a higher defense budget.
choices.B: Cooperate with allies to pool resources.
choices.C: Delay and invest in the economy first.

wp_178.title: Sensitive Export Contract
wp_178.body: A company wants to export sensitive technology. Big money… but big concern.

choices.A: Authorize it with strict controls and clauses.
choices.B: Refuse: sovereignty and security first.
choices.C: Authorize it only to selected allies.

wp_179.title: Aggressive TV Interview
wp_179.body: A major network is preparing a 'no gloves' interview. Your staff is already looking for a helmet.

choices.A: Go on: transparency and education.
choices.B: Refuse: you speak to the people another way.
choices.C: Offer a longer, calmer format elsewhere.

wp_180.title: Satellite Accident
wp_180.body: A small national satellite fails. The media call it a 'space defeat.'

choices.A: Restart the space program, though expensive.
choices.B: Buy services abroad, faster option.
choices.C: International cooperation + cost sharing.

wp_181.title: Medicine Shortage
wp_181.body: Common medicines are missing. People are stockpiling. Pharmacies are sighing in unison.

choices.A: Relocate part of production, though costly.
choices.B: Emergency import deals + strategic stockpiles.
choices.C: Price controls + priority for hospitals.

wp_182.title: Ministry Hack
wp_182.body: A ministry website displays a banner that says 'hello.' Not an official hello.

choices.A: Full audit + cyber training for government staff.
choices.B: Cyber retaliation + firm communication.
choices.C: Outsource critical infrastructure to a certified operator.

wp_183.title: Tourism Recovery
wp_183.body: Tourism is down. An adviser suggests: 'put a giant panda on the posters.'

choices.A: Tourism plan + simpler visas.
choices.B: Major international event, risky but visible.
choices.C: Low-key campaign + better infrastructure.

wp_184.title: University Exchange
wp_184.body: A country offers 10,000 exchange scholarships. Win-win… except for your budget.

choices.A: Accept and co-fund: soft power.
choices.B: Accept, but reduce the volume and target it.
choices.C: Refuse: internal finances come first.

wp_185.title: Legendary Traffic Jams
wp_185.body: Traffic jams become legendary. One mayor says: 'we should open a museum for cars standing still.'

choices.A: Invest in public transport + incentives.
choices.B: Strong measures: urban tolls, though unpopular.
choices.C: Road and parking plan: quick solution.

wp_186.title: Fictional Climate Conference
wp_186.body: You are offered the chance to host a conference on air and water. Very consensual… until funding comes up.

choices.A: Host it and invest: international image.
choices.B: Host it without public spending.
choices.C: Refuse: national priorities first.

wp_187.title: Veterans' Protest
wp_187.body: Veterans demand better support. Media headlines ask: 'respect or neglect?'

choices.A: Increase resources and dedicated services.
choices.B: Administrative reform with no extra budget.
choices.C: Program with partners and supervised philanthropy.

wp_188.title: National Lottery Debate
wp_188.body: The lottery funds public projects. Some call it 'government by scratch ticket.'

choices.A: Keep it, but regulate it better and show transparency.
choices.B: Cut it back sharply: ethics first.
choices.C: Expand it: fund infrastructure quickly.

wp_189.title: Twin Cities Summit
wp_189.body: Twin cities want a major summit. One mayor wants 'a giant flag.'

choices.A: Support it: local diplomacy and exchanges.
choices.B: Support it, but with no budget: symbol only.
choices.C: Refuse: refocus on national diplomacy.

wp_190.title: Sports Data Leak
wp_190.body: Data from a public sports program leaks. People discover the budget for 'balls' is huge.

choices.A: Transparency + audit + correction.
choices.B: Immediate sanctions and firm communication.
choices.C: Digital reform + security investment.

wp_191.title: Cyber Defense Pact
wp_191.body: An ally proposes a cyber defense pact. Advantage: protection. Downside: dependence.

choices.A: Sign: stronger security.
choices.B: Sign, but keep strong national autonomy.
choices.C: Refuse to preserve cyber autonomy.

wp_192.title: Satirical Newspaper
wp_192.body: A satirical paper caricatures you as a penguin. Very elegant, very humiliating.

choices.A: Laugh and invite the cartoonist to calm things down.
choices.B: Sue, even if it creates tension.
choices.C: Ignore it and launch a concrete popular action.

wp_193.title: Cracked Strategic Bridge
wp_193.body: A major bridge is showing cracks. Truckers are inventing spontaneous prayers.

choices.A: Repair it fast: big budget, safety first.
choices.B: Reroute traffic and make a long-term plan.
choices.C: Get help from allies + military engineers to speed it up.

wp_194.title: Trade tension
wp_194.body: A partner announces new trade barriers. Officially, it is an adjustment. In reality, it is a targeted attack.

choices.A: Negotiate calmly and propose a compromise.
choices.B: Respond with retaliatory measures.
choices.C: Quickly diversify toward other partners.

wp_195.title: Queue Crisis
wp_195.body: Government offices have monstrous waiting lines. One citizen says: 'I grew up here.'

choices.A: Digitize quickly + temporary hiring.
choices.B: Strict controls and targets, commando style.
choices.C: Outsource part of the services, though risky.

wp_196.title: Contested Military Parade
wp_196.body: A military parade is planned. Some want pride, others want a social budget.

choices.A: Keep it, but cut the cost and explain it.
choices.B: Cancel it and reallocate funds to public services.
choices.C: Keep it and invite partners as a message of alliance.

wp_197.title: VIP Airport Lounge Incident
wp_197.body: An incident in the VIP lounge goes viral. Even the moving walkways have an opinion.

choices.A: Apologies + security audit + fast improvements.
choices.B: Publicly punish management.
choices.C: Hand management to a specialized agency.

wp_198.title: The 'Everything Free' Syndrome
wp_198.body: A rumor announces totally free public transport. It is false… but very popular.

choices.A: Deny it + propose real targeted aid.
choices.B: Test free transport on weekends, though expensive.
choices.C: Ignore the rumor and strengthen inspections.

wp_296.body: A consulate is vandalized. You must avoid escalation.

choices.A: Condemn it + cooperate to find those responsible.
choices.B: Expulsions and firm measures.
choices.C: Mediation and symbolic gestures of peace.

wp_297.title: The Promise of a 'Total Shield'
wp_297.body: An industrialist tries to sell you a total shield. Your expert has doubts.

choices.A: Fund controlled research, stay cautious.
choices.B: Buy it fast: show power.
choices.C: Refuse and invest in classic layered defense.

wp_298.title: Coffee Diplomacy
wp_298.body: A humanitarian ship drifts toward a tense zone. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Accept it, peace comes first.
choices.B: Refuse: no tantrums.
choices.C: Accept it, but on neutral ground.

wp_299.title: The Fake Statement
wp_299.body: A virtual summit is supposed to calm a conflict. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Immediate denial + stronger cyber defenses.
choices.B: Accuse the rival: sanctions and firm posture.
choices.C: Create a joint anti-disinformation protocol.

wp_300.title: The Mechanical Dove
wp_300.body: You are offered a so-called green deterrence system. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Laugh it off, own it, and announce a peace initiative.
choices.B: Cancel the event and stay firm.
choices.C: Reschedule it: symbol + diplomatic agreement.

wp_301.title: Prolonged Drought

choices.A: Nationwide restrictions + aid to affected sectors.
choices.B: Invest heavily in water: networks, reservoirs, and recycling.
choices.C: Supply agreements with partners.

wp_302.title: Exceptional Flooding

choices.A: Emergency plan + logistics reinforcements and faster repairs.
choices.B: Prioritize prevention, levees, and standards.
choices.C: Mobilize international aid for equipment and expertise.

wp_303.title: Heat Wave and Power Demand
wp_303.body: Demand for gas rises sharply. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Energy-saving measures + targeted aid for households.
choices.B: Speed up production and storage, despite the cost.
choices.C: Energy imports and temporary deals with neighbors.

wp_304.title: Storm and Mobile Network
wp_304.body: A storm damages antennas and the mobile network becomes unstable. The local economy slows down.

choices.A: Fast repair plan with priority for key areas.
choices.B: Invest in resilience with backups and redundancy.
choices.C: Public-private partnership to move faster and share the cost.

wp_305.title: Forest Fires
wp_305.body: Fires are increasing. Local authorities want more prevention and response resources.

choices.A: Strengthen firefighting resources + national coordination.
choices.B: Long-term plan: prevention, brush clearing, land planning.
choices.C: Regional cooperation: shared air resources and drills.

wp_306.title: Landslide

choices.C: Insurance and private incentives to speed up prevention.

wp_308.title: Tension Over Cereals

choices.C: Diversified trade agreements to secure supply.

choices.C: International negotiations to stabilize access to resources.

wp_310.title: Supply Chain Breakdown

choices.A: Keep it with stronger logistics and adaptation.
choices.B: Postpone it to secure organization and image.
choices.C: Turn it into a reduced and more controlled format.

wp_394.title: Aid Requested by an Ally
wp_394.body: An ally is dealing with fires and asks for help. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Send logistical aid and immediate coordination.
choices.B: Limited aid: material support, no heavy deployment.
choices.C: Propose a regional coalition of shared resources.

wp_395.title: Disaster Abroad
wp_395.body: A partner suffers a disaster and asks for quick aid.

choices.A: Fast and visible aid, with diplomatic coordination.
choices.B: Quiet and targeted support to limit costs.
choices.C: Aid conditioned on an economic cooperation agreement.

wp_396.title: Contribution to a Global Fund
wp_396.body: A climate fund asks for more resources. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Increase the contribution and gain diplomatic influence.
choices.B: Maintain the current level and focus efforts internally.
choices.C: Contribute through targeted and measurable projects.

wp_397.title: Trade Agreement to Renegotiate
wp_397.body: A trade agreement must be renegotiated. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Renegotiate to open things up more, with targeted protections.
choices.B: Tighten the agreement to protect sensitive sectors.
choices.C: Move forward in stages with regular review.

wp_398.title: Maritime Incident
wp_398.body: An incident at sea creates tension over fishing and shipping. The images are everywhere.

choices.A: De-escalation: immediate talks and a rules framework.
choices.B: Show of presence and tighter controls.
choices.C: Coalition with allies for joint surveillance.

wp_399.title: Humanitarian Supply
wp_399.body: Organizations are asking for logistical aid in a difficult zone. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: National logistical support for transport and coordination.
choices.B: Financial aid to specialized actors, no heavy deployment.
choices.C: Joint operation with partners to pool resources.

wp_400.title: Lost International Arbitration
wp_400.body: An international ruling penalizes a national player. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Respect the ruling and negotiate exit conditions.
choices.B: Appeal and prepare targeted trade measures.
choices.C: Support the company through economic diplomacy and diversification.

wp_401.title: Flooding and Traffic
wp_401.body: Heavy rains are making water levels rise. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Deploy emergency resources and secure critical points.
choices.B: 2-year flood-control plan with levees, basins, and alerts.
choices.C: Request technical and material support from partners.

wp_402.title: Prolonged Heat Wave
wp_402.body: The heat lasts and demand for water and energy rises. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Temporary restrictions + aid to the most exposed sectors.
choices.B: Adaptation plan for urban shade, buildings, and the network.
choices.C: Pool water and energy with the region.

wp_403.title: Blocked Supply
wp_403.body: A logistical incident slows imports. Some prices are rising.

choices.A: Immediate unblocking plan + priority for essential goods.
choices.B: Invest in redundancy through rail, ports, and warehouses.
choices.C: Negotiate alternative routes with trade partners.

wp_404.title: Trade Corridor Under Tension
wp_404.body: A trade corridor is becoming tense. Economic players want it secured.

choices.A: Strengthen convoy protection and surveillance.
choices.B: Quickly diversify routes and suppliers, even if more expensive.
choices.C: Create a diplomatic coalition to stabilize the zone.

wp_405.title: Border Blockage
wp_405.body: New formalities are slowing crossings. Trucks are piling up.

choices.A: Rapid negotiation to simplify procedures.
choices.B: Reciprocal measures: tighter controls on the national side.
choices.C: Domestic logistical aid + faster digitization of procedures.

wp_406.title: Transport Cost Surge
wp_406.body: Transport costs keep rising for months. Small businesses say their margins are being crushed.

choices.A: Temporary targeted aid for strategic small businesses.
choices.B: Logistics efficiency plan with rail, pooling, and hubs.
choices.C: Trade agreements to stabilize part of the rates.

wp_407.title: Component Shortage
wp_407.body: Key components are becoming scarce. Industry is slowing down and wants a rapid response.

choices.A: Secure supply through long-term contracts.
choices.B: Invest in local production and technological alternatives.
choices.C: Agreements with allies to pool purchases and stockpiles.

wp_408.title: Drought and Agriculture
wp_408.body: Drought lowers some yields. Prices are still stable, but the sector is worried.

choices.A: Adaptation aid on irrigation, storage, and crop varieties.
choices.B: Water arbitration: temporary quotas and tighter controls.
choices.C: Temporary imports to stabilize prices.

wp_409.title: Risk to a Levee
wp_409.body: A report flags a risk to a major levee. Action is needed fast.

choices.A: Immediate emergency works, safety first.
choices.B: Full consolidation plan with multi-year funding.
choices.C: External audit and phased works.

wp_410.title: Tension on the Markets
wp_410.body: Strong volatility worries households and businesses. Investors want reassurance.

choices.A: Announce a stable and predictable budget framework.
choices.B: Temporary measures to protect the most exposed savings.
choices.C: Coordinate with partners to reassure and stabilize.

wp_411.title: Tax Conflict Over Digital
wp_411.body: A partner criticizes your digital taxation and threatens action. The sector fears escalation.

choices.A: Negotiate an international compromise and partially harmonize.
choices.B: Stay firm and accept reciprocal measures.
choices.C: Offer targeted exemptions to avoid a trade war.

wp_412.title: Pharmaceutical Stock Shortage
wp_412.body: Medicines are temporarily missing. Stakeholders want the supply chain secured.

choices.A: Emergency purchases and priority use for essential needs.
choices.B: Partial relocation and strategic stockpiles.
choices.C: International agreements on sharing and coordinated production.

wp_413.title: Tension Over Access to Metals
wp_413.body: Industrial metals are becoming more expensive. Industrial players want a strategy to secure supply.

choices.A: Diversify suppliers and sign long-term contracts.
choices.B: Invest in recycling, substitution, and material restraint.
choices.C: Create a purchasing alliance with partners.

wp_414.title: Localized Flooding
wp_414.body: Urban areas are hit by flooding. Local services want national support.

choices.A: Emergency support + rapid repair fund.
choices.B: City adaptation plan with drainage, soil, and alerts.
choices.C: Technical cooperation with experienced foreign cities.

wp_415.title: Aggressive Competition
wp_415.body: A country subsidizes a sector where your companies are strong. Your market share is under threat.

choices.A: Targeted temporary support + complaint through international channels.
choices.B: Speed up innovation and move upmarket to rely less on price.
choices.C: Negotiate a limitation or compensation agreement.

wp_416.title: Economy and Interest Rates
wp_416.body: The cost of credit is rising. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Targeted guarantees for small businesses and strategic projects.
choices.B: Reduce some spending to reassure the markets.
choices.C: Targeted public investment plan to support demand.

wp_417.title: Pollution in a Waterway
wp_417.body: Pollution forces limits on certain uses. Experts want to act fast.

choices.A: Stronger controls + immediate cleanup plan.
choices.B: Reform standards and impose stronger sanctions, at the risk of tensions.
choices.C: Invest in water and sanitation.

wp_418.title: Contested Public Procurement
wp_418.body: A partner restricts access to its public contracts. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Diplomatic and legal negotiation, without escalation.
choices.B: Reciprocity: restrict some access in return.
choices.C: Domestic compensation: support exports to other markets.

wp_419.title: Risk of Logistical Avalanches
wp_419.body: Seasonal risks disrupt deliveries in the mountains. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Emergency plan and prioritize essential goods.
choices.B: Invest in prevention, galleries, and sensors.
choices.C: Pool a permanent system with neighboring regions.

wp_420.title: Export Platform Incident
wp_420.body: An incident reduces the capacity of an export platform. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Fast repair + temporary continuity plan.
choices.B: Full modernization and security upgrades, more costly.
choices.C: Temporary agreements to absorb the flow.

wp_421.title: Conflict Over Product Standards
wp_421.body: A partner imposes new standards against your exporters. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Negotiate a transition period and mutual recognition.
choices.B: Retaliate: equivalent controls and standards on your side.
choices.C: Help businesses comply with the standards.

wp_422.title: Global Slowdown
wp_422.body: Global indicators are worsening. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Targeted, temporary, and measured support plan.
choices.B: Strict budget discipline to protect credibility.
choices.C: International coordination for a joint plan.

wp_423.title: Pressure on Energy
wp_423.body: Several facilities need modernization. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Speed up works with stronger oversight and transparency.
choices.B: Slow down to prioritize consultation and safety.
choices.C: International partnership to share expertise and financing.

wp_424.title: Unstable Transport Prices
wp_424.body: Transport prices remain unstable. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Temporary targeted aid + goals to lower costs.
choices.B: Reform the sector with regulation, competition, and investment.
choices.C: Negotiate long-term contracts to secure prices.

wp_425.title: Shock to Agricultural Insurance
wp_425.body: Agricultural insurance premiums are rising sharply. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Temporarily subsidize part of the premiums.
choices.B: Create a national reinsurance mechanism.
choices.C: Invest in prevention and adaptation to reduce risk.

wp_426.title: Imports Rising Fast
wp_426.body: Imports are growing faster than exports. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: 2-year competitiveness plan: innovation, energy, simplification.
choices.B: Targeted protective measures on sensitive sectors.
choices.C: Trade agreements to improve access to new markets.

wp_427.title: Low Business Confidence
wp_427.body: Businesses are delaying investment because they lack visibility. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Announce a stable tax and regulatory path.
choices.B: Immediate targeted investment incentives.
choices.C: Major social and economic pact with partners and unions.

wp_428.title: Pressure on Rail Freight
wp_428.body: Rail freight is saturated. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Invest urgently in capacity and maintenance.
choices.B: Open the market further to competition under strict rules.
choices.C: Logistics agreements with neighbors to distribute the flows.

wp_429.title: Tension Over Gas Prices
wp_429.body: Gas prices fluctuate sharply. Pressure is mounting.

choices.A: Temporary targeted shield + supervised restraint.
choices.B: Diversify contracts and strengthen strategic reserves.
choices.C: Speed up alternatives through investment.

wp_430.title: Infrastructure Damage
wp_430.body: A weather event damages roads and networks. Regions are asking for fast action.

choices.A: Unlock an emergency fund and prioritize repairs.
choices.B: Launch a more ambitious and durable resilience plan.
choices.C: Mixed financing under public control.

wp_431.title: Agri-Food Dispute
wp_431.body: A partner blocks agri-food products. Supply chains want a reaction.

choices.A: Immediate negotiation + health and technical guarantees.
choices.B: Targeted retaliation on an equivalent sector.
choices.C: Diversify export markets and support sector transition.

wp_432.title: Tension Over Quotas
wp_432.body: Import quotas are reduced abroad. Your companies lose key access.

choices.A: Legal and diplomatic action through existing channels.
choices.B: Alternative bilateral agreements, with limited concessions.
choices.C: Support commercial conversion toward other markets.

wp_433.title: Strategic Project Blocked
wp_433.body: A strategic industrial project is delayed by legal appeals. Investors want a decision.

choices.A: Speed up procedures while keeping a control framework.
choices.B: Maintain the full process, acceptability first.
choices.C: Scale down the project and help the affected people.

wp_434.title: Conflict Over Export Licenses
wp_434.body: A partner limits export licenses on some equipment. Your companies are blocked.

choices.A: Negotiate and secure targeted exemptions.
choices.B: Reduce dependence: develop a national alternative.
choices.C: Coalition with allies for coordinated pressure.

wp_435.title: Food Storage Crisis
wp_435.body: Warehouses lack capacity after disruptions. Some products are becoming scarce.

choices.A: Emergency plan: prioritization + temporary logistics.
choices.B: Invest in strategic stockpiles and storage capacity.
choices.C: Agreements with distributors to optimize and pool resources.

wp_436.title: Industrial Property Dispute
wp_436.body: A partner accuses your companies of violating patents. The threat of trade sanctions hangs over you.

choices.A: Launch mediation and set up a temporary license.
choices.B: Challenge it firmly and prepare countermeasures.
choices.C: Create a bilateral framework for quick dispute resolution.

wp_437.title: Construction Cost Surge
wp_437.body: Construction materials are getting more expensive. Projects are slowing and local officials want support.

choices.A: Targeted aid for priority projects and renovation.
choices.B: Launch a national industrial plan for key materials.
choices.C: Negotiate group purchasing with several partners.

wp_438.title: Public Procurement and Competition
wp_438.body: Some players denounce a lack of competition in certain public contracts. Trust is falling.

choices.A: Stronger transparency and anti-fraud oversight.
choices.B: Make public contracts easier for small businesses to access.
choices.C: Full reform with tougher sanctions, even if it causes tension.

wp_439.title: Regional Imbalance
wp_439.body: Some regions say they have received less aid after climate shocks. They want a fair mechanism.

choices.A: Create a stable territorial solidarity fund.
choices.B: Distribute case by case based on measured economic impact.
choices.C: Decentralize more resources and responsibilities to the regions.

wp_440.title: Tense Economic Cooperation
wp_440.body: A partner wants to renegotiate a major economic agreement. It wants more, or it will reduce cooperation.

choices.A: Renegotiate while keeping clear red lines.
choices.B: Refuse and prepare an economic substitution plan.
choices.C: Propose a multi-step agreement with a planned review.

wp_441.title: Labor Market: Shortage
wp_441.body: Essential sectors are short on workers. Costs are rising and delays are getting longer.

choices.A: Fast training plan + better conditions.
choices.B: Make regulated international hiring easier.
choices.C: Speed up automation and productivity gains.

wp_442.title: Pressure on Research
wp_442.body: A partner wants to limit scientific exchanges in certain fields. Your researchers are worried.

choices.A: Negotiate a secure framework to keep exchanges going.
choices.B: Develop a more expensive autonomous national program.
choices.C: Create a research alliance with several partners.

wp_443.title: Uncertainty on the Project
wp_443.body: A major project is running late. Costs are rising and public opinion wants answers.

choices.A: Transparency + revised timeline and stronger oversight.
choices.B: Speed things up by mobilizing exceptional resources.
choices.C: Reduce the scope to stay within budget and deadlines.

wp_444.title: Pressure on Water
wp_444.body: Water-intensive industries want guarantees. Citizens want essentials first.

choices.A: Prioritize essential uses + temporary quotas for industry.
choices.B: Invest in reuse and water-saving technologies.
choices.C: Regulated temporary supply and transfer agreements.

wp_445.title: Blocked Agricultural Markets
wp_445.body: A partner limits access for agricultural products. Export sectors want a solution.

choices.A: Negotiate common standards and checks to lift the restriction.
choices.B: Targeted retaliation on an equivalent economic sector.
choices.C: Support diversification of outlets and higher quality positioning.

wp_446.title: Pressure on Water Prices
wp_446.body: The cost of treating and distributing water is rising. Users are worried.

choices.A: Targeted aid to local authorities + anti-leak plan.
choices.B: Gradual tariff reform with social protections.
choices.C: Massive national investment to reduce long-term costs.

wp_447.title: Shock to Road Freight
wp_447.body: Road freight costs are rising because of outside pressures. Companies want an alternative.

choices.A: Temporary aid + logistics optimization plan.
choices.B: Speed up modal shift to rail and waterways and multimodal hubs.
choices.C: Negotiate a regional agreement to stabilize conditions.

wp_448.title: Doubt Over Tech Exports
wp_448.body: A partner wants to restrict exports of key technologies. Your industrial sector fears shortages.

choices.A: Negotiate exemptions and supply guarantees.
choices.B: Create an alternative national or allied supply chain.
choices.C: Mutualization agreements and strategic stockpiles with allies.

wp_449.title: Pressure on Local Budgets
wp_449.body: After several climate shocks, cities want budget support. Public finances are tight.

choices.A: Targeted support fund with strict criteria.
choices.B: Low-interest loans and technical support instead of subsidies.
choices.C: Decentralize tax resources to strengthen autonomy.

wp_450.title: Urgent Economic Agreement
wp_450.body: Several sectors are asking for a clearer resilience doctrine.

choices.A: Sign with minimum guarantees and a quick review clause.
choices.B: Refuse and activate an internal substitution plan.
choices.C: Negotiate for 48 more hours to get a better balance.
